summary: C2 backdoor beacons hardcoded IP and executes attacker commands on require()
details: On require(), index.js runs a top-level IIFE that contacts a hardcoded command-and-control endpoint at http://98.86.244.177:8080 over plaintext HTTP. The flow registers the host by POSTing os.hostname() and process.platform to /register, GETs /beacon to receive an attacker-supplied shell command, executes it via child_process.exec, and POSTs the captured stdout/stderr back to /results. This is a fully functional remote-access backdoor: any developer or build system that installs and loads friendly-greeter-demo immediately leaks host identifiers and runs arbitrary commands chosen by the operator of 98.86.244.177. The package's stated purpose ('tiny educational greeter') is a cover; an in-file comment explicitly labels the payload a 'C2 BEACON', and package.json carries placeholder author metadata ('Your Name <salman.ta12312@gmail.com>') inconsistent with a published library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
